mnemonics: SPACE: Spaces, Paragraphs, And Comments Erased (by compiler)
keyPoints: Whitespace (spaces, tabs, newlines) is used to separate tokens in code
The Java compiler ignores excess whitespace, using it only for readability
Whitespace does not affect program execution but improves human understanding
commonMistakes: Adding whitespace within identifiers (e.g., 'my Variable' instead of 'myVariable')
Inconsistent indentation making code harder to read and maintain
Placing meaningful code on the same line after a comment (// comment codeShouldBeOnNewLine)
